codeReview.summary: The feature correctly fans Claude scans out to `~/.claude/skills` plus marketplace subdirectories, but commit `5792ed3` routes marketplace results through the normal `skills` and `skill_installations` tables. That makes marketplace rows manageable installs and collapses duplicate user/marketplace copies, violating the mission's required observational-only behavior.

issues.description: Every scanned Claude root is persisted through `db::upsert_skill` and `db::upsert_skill_installation`, so marketplace hits become ordinary install-state rows. With the existing agent/detail queries (`src-tauri/src/db.rs:930-944`, `src-tauri/src/commands/skills.rs:64-149`), marketplace copies show up as normal manageable installs instead of read-only observational rows. The feature's own test at `src-tauri/src/commands/scanner.rs:916-928` locks in that incorrect behavior by asserting installation records for marketplace skills.

The storage path still keys logical skills by `skills.id` (`ON CONFLICT(id) DO UPDATE`) and installations by `(skill_id, agent_id)` (`src-tauri/src/db.rs:995-998`). Once Claude scans both `~/.claude/skills` and marketplace roots, duplicate logical skills cannot remain distinct: later scans overwrite earlier rows and normal agent queries (`src-tauri/src/db.rs:930-944`) can only return one row per skill ID. That breaks the mission requirement that user and marketplace copies survive as separate Claude-owned rows.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend worker skill/procedure lacks guidance for producing a scoped feature commit when the shared mission workspace is already dirty from unrelated work. The worker had to deviate from the environment-cleanup step to avoid disturbing other in-progress features.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff `/Users/happypeet/.factory/missions/a998172c-79b0-469d-a170-fcefe54fb0eb/handoffs/2026-04-21T14-08-49-224Z__claude-source-root-discovery__c8b63ef6-8cb2-43da-a1ef-2090a3c689d5.json` records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure = false` with a deviation for step `3.2 Environment Cleanup`; mission AGENTS.md also notes the shared repo may already contain unrelated dirty files.

summary: Review failed. Claude root discovery itself was added, but the implementation persisted marketplace scans as normal install rows and continued using single-key skill/install storage, so marketplace rows are not observational-only and duplicate user/marketplace copies cannot stay distinct.
